title: In-Flight Promise Deduplication, Waiters, Abort Ownership, and Cleanup · Compiler Case: Pokedex Ts Independent Waiter Abort
summary: Concurrent misses create duplicate fetches, a shared promise retains one caller signal, first cancellation rejects all waiters, and settled entries remain in the in-flight map. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts independent waiter abort increment in waiter-aware duplicate-suppressed async loader. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Allow a waiter to stop observing without necessarily aborting work required by others and cancel shared work only under policy.
Give the loader its own controller and define waiter registration, completion, publication, and final cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Independent Waiter Abort
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 3587 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one shared load, independent waiter abort, safe publication, rejection handling, and cleanup; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Allow a waiter to stop observing without necessarily aborting work required by others and cancel shared work only under policy.
